channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Persian Gulf States Navigate Strategic Challenges Post-Iran Conflict
item.description: Once the US-Israeli conflict with Iran concludes, Persian Gulf countries will confront a complex strategic environment. Despite aligning with the US for security and engaging diplomatically with Iran ...

item.title: Senators raise security concerns over Islamabad Safe City surveillance system
item.description: In Islamabad, the Senate Standing Committee on Information Technology raised concerns about the security of the city’s Safe City surveillance system. The discussion was prompted by recent reports of t...

item.title: WNBA and Players’ Union Continue Negotiations Amid Key Disagreements on Revenue and Benefits
item.description: The WNBA and its players’ union resumed negotiations on Wednesday following a 12-hour marathon session that ended early Wednesday morning without an agreement on a new collective bargaining agreement ...

item.title: Tanzania Strengthens Laboratory Network to Improve Disease Detection and Preparedness
item.description: Tanzania is moving forward with efforts to make its health system stronger by improving disease surveillance and laboratory coordination. On March 11, 2026, health experts, government officials, regul...

item.title: Arno Greeff steps away from acting to focus on family and new pursuits
item.description: Veteran South African actor Arno Greeff has announced that he is stepping back from long-form television and film after a 12-year career. In an honest Instagram post, Greeff explained that over the pa...

item.title: Leo Sayer reflects on his career, financial betrayal and extraordinary celebrity encounters
item.description: In a candid interview, veteran British-Australian singer Leo Sayer, now 77 and living in Australia, shares highlights from his remarkable life and career. He rose to fame in the 1970s with hits such a...

item.title: The Dahiyeh Doctrine Returns: Israel's Disproportionate Force Strategy Applied Again in Beirut's Southern Suburb Amid Renewed Conflict with Hezbollah
item.description: Beirut’s southern suburb of Dahiyeh, a key Hezbollah stronghold, has once again become a primary target for Israeli airstrikes in the renewed Middle East conflict that escalated over the past ten days...

item.title: US Announces Permanent Closure of Peshawar Consulate, Services Shifted to Islamabad
item.description: The United States has confirmed it will permanently close its consulate in Peshawar, which has historically been the closest American diplomatic post to the Afghan border and a key operations hub sinc...

item.title: Kenya Schools Explore AI in Drama Scripts Amid Ethical Debate
item.description: In Kenya, the National Drama and Film Festival is seeing a rise in the use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) by scriptwriters to improve their creative works. This development has sparked debate on the ...

item.title: Iran Confirms Withdrawal from 2026 World Cup Amid Political Tensions
item.description: Iran's participation in the 2026 World Cup has been officially ruled out by the country's sports minister, Ahmad Donyamali. This comes amid escalating regional tensions following attacks by US and Isr...

item.title: Rising Gas Prices in Canada Linked to Middle East Conflict
item.description: Gas prices across Canada have surged due to escalating tensions in the Middle East, specifically the conflict involving the U.S., Israel, and Iran. The instability in the region has caused oil prices ...

item.title: Dakshina Kannada Man Arrested for Unlicensed Diesel Hoarding Over 4,000 Litres
item.description: In Dakshina Kannada district, a 35-year-old man named Jagasish was apprehended for hoarding 4,175 litres of diesel without a licence. The police recovered the fuel from 20 drums of 200 litres each and...

item.title: Former Marines and Lawyer Baligod Cooperate with NBI on Cash Delivery Allegations
item.description: Lawyer Levito Baligod, along with several alleged former Marines, appeared before the National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) on Thursday regarding claims that they delivered cash from former lawmaker ...

item.title: Bulls gear up for intense derby clash with Stormers as Van Staden nears 100th game
item.description: The Pretoria-based Bulls are preparing for a fiery United Rugby Championship derby against their old rivals, the Stormers, at Loftus Versfeld this Saturday, with kickoff set for 2pm. Captain Marcell C...

item.title: Report warns high blood pressure affects many Nigerian adults
item.description: According to the State of Health of the Nation Report 2025 wey dem release for Abuja, hypertension dey affect about 35 to 40 per cent of Nigerian adults. Di report talk say even though awareness and s...

item.title: Suzuki Across Mild-Hybrid SUV Prices and Features Announced for South Africa
item.description: Suzuki has revealed pricing and specifications for its all-new Across SUV in South Africa, ahead of the media launch scheduled for March 17–19. Built by Maruti Suzuki in India, where it is called the ...

item.title: Tellus Minerals B2B Platform Connects Tanzanian Artisanal Miners to International Buyers
item.description: Dar es Salaam-based Tellus Minerals has launched a digital B2B marketplace aimed at empowering Tanzania’s artisanal and small-scale miners by connecting them directly with international buyers, invest...

item.title: Jake Paul Appears on Stage With Donald Trump Amid Wardrobe Mishap
item.description: Jake Paul joined former U.S. President Donald Trump on stage at a campaign rally in Hebron, Kentucky, on Thursday evening. The event, held at the Verst Logistics facility, featured Trump praising his ...

item.title: Majority of surveyed GPs report never denying fit notes for mental health reasons amid rising sickness absences
item.description: A BBC survey of more than 5,000 GPs in England revealed that, of the 752 who responded, 540 had never refused to issue a fit note – commonly known as a sick note – to a patient citing mental health is...

item.title: Why Nairobi keeps flooding and what the city must fix
item.description: 'The day when Nairobi drowns' is an opinion piece that talks about how Nairobi floods again and again whenever it rains heavily, exposing the city’s poor planning and weak drainage systems. The writer...
